title: Remove unused getLogoPath and getPreviewDate functions
description:
The functions `getLogoPath()` and `getPreviewDate()` in src/utils/logoUtils.ts are defined but never used anywhere in the codebase.

## Details
- `getLogoPath()` is unused - SeasonalLogo component builds its own logo path inline
- `getPreviewDate()` is unused
- `parseISO` from date-fns is imported solely for `getPreviewDate()`

## Proposed Solution
Remove both functions and the `parseISO` import to eliminate dead code and reduce bundle size.

title: Consolidate duplicate CSS definitions across stylesheets
description:
Multiple CSS rules are duplicated across files, causing maintenance burden and potential specificity conflicts.

## Duplicates Found
- `.page` styles: index.css (lines 140-144), layout.css (lines 31-36)
- `.page-title` styles: index.css (lines 146-149), layout.css (lines 38-43)
- `.submit-button` styles: index.css (lines 161-173), contact.css (lines 102-124)
- `body` styles: index.css (lines 8-14), base.css (lines 20-27)
- `html, body, #root` height/width: index.css (lines 16-22), layout.css (lines 1-9)
- `.main-content` styles: index.css (lines 102-109), layout.css (lines 23-28)
- `.app-container` styles: index.css (lines 28-32), layout.css (lines 12-20)
- `.logo` styles: index.css (lines 126-138) vs App.css (lines 8-19) - conflicting definitions

## Proposed Solution
Consolidate duplicate rules into single authoritative locations. Remove duplicates from index.css where they already exist in dedicated style files.

title: Remove unused App.css file
description:
src/App.css contains Vite boilerplate CSS (logo spinning animation, .card, .read-the-docs) that appears unused by the application. The .logo class defined here conflicts with the .logo class in index.css.

## Proposed Solution
Remove App.css entirely if none of its styles are used. If the .logo hover effect is desired, consolidate it into index.css.

Set up TypeScript configuration (tsconfig.json, dependencies)
Update Jest config for TypeScript and convert tests to .tsx
Convert src/utils/logoUtils.js to TypeScript with proper date types
Convert src/components/SeasonalLogo.jsx to TSX with props interface
Convert src/components/Header.jsx to TSX with props interface
Convert src/components/Footer.jsx to TSX
Convert src/pages/Home.jsx to TSX (includes AnimatedBird component)
Convert src/pages/Contact.jsx to TSX with form types
Convert src/pages/About.jsx and Crops.jsx to TSX
Convert src/App.jsx and main.jsx to TSX
Add Helmet SEO metadata to Home and Contact pages
description:
The About and Crops pages include <Helmet> for SEO metadata, but Home and Contact do not.

## Proposed Solution
Add consistent <Helmet> tags to Home.tsx and Contact.tsx pages for SEO consistency across the site.

title: CSS cleanup: remove commented code and redundant font-family declarations
description:
Minor CSS cleanup items:

## 1. Commented-out CSS code
src/index.css (lines 191-199) has a commented-out `.navbar-logo` rule that appears unused.

## 2. Redundant font-family declarations
`font-family: var(--font-body)` is declared in base.css on the body element, which should cascade to all elements. Yet it is repeatedly redeclared on .app-container, .footer, .contact-form, .helper-text, .submit-button, .chirp-bubble, and .home-subtitle (10 occurrences across 5 files).

## Proposed Solution
- Remove commented-out code
- Remove redundant font-family declarations that inherit from body

Add keyboard support to AnimatedBird component

Add aria-current=page to active navigation links

Code Quality & Consolidation

Replace hardcoded colors in index.css and contact.css with CSS variables
Consolidate duplicate cloud animation CSS - keep only in home.css
Move navbar styles from index.css to dedicated navbar.css
Remove unused useState/useEffect imports from App.jsx
Remove console.log from Contact.jsx and replace alert() with toast notification
Refactor Header navigation links to use data mapping
description:
The navigation links in Header.tsx (lines 46-77) repeat the same pattern four times with only the path and label changing.

## Current State
Each link has identical onClick, className, and aria-current logic repeated.

## Proposed Solution
Extract navigation items into a constant array and map over it:
```tsx
const NAV_ITEMS = [
  { path: '/', label: 'Home' },
  { path: '/about', label: 'About' },
  { path: '/crops', label: 'Crops' },
  { path: '/contact', label: 'Contact' },
];
```
Then render via .map(). This reduces ~30 lines to ~10 lines and makes adding/removing nav items trivial.

title: Refactor Home component goose images to reduce duplication
description:
Three goose images in Home.tsx (lines 50-79) share nearly identical structure with only minor differences (image source, className modifier, alt text, click handler).

## Proposed Solution
Define goose data as an array and map over it, or extract a GooseImage component that accepts props for the variations. This reduces code duplication and improves maintainability.

title: Contact Form Backend (Lambda + SES)
Set up AWS SES identity and verify coatesvillefarm@gmail.com as recipient
Create Lambda function for contact form submission (Node.js/TypeScript)
Add rate limiting to Lambda (max 5 submissions per IP per hour)
Set up API Gateway endpoint for Lambda with CORS for coatesvillefarm.com
Update Contact.tsx to POST to Lambda API endpoint
Add loading state and error handling to contact form submission
Optional: Add reCAPTCHA v3 invisible verification for additional bot protection
Create infrastructure-as-code (CDK or SAM template) for Lambda/API Gateway
Fix undefined CSS variable --color-white
description:
src/styles/home.css (line 23) uses `var(--color-white)` but this variable is not defined in variables.css. Only `--color-surface: #ffffff` is defined.

## Proposed Solution
Either:
1. Replace `var(--color-white)` with `var(--color-surface)`, or
2. Add `--color-white: #ffffff` to variables.css

title: Standardize CSS variable naming (--color-bg vs --color-background)
description:
Both `--color-background` and `--color-bg` are defined in variables.css with the same value (#f1fce9). The codebase uses both interchangeably.

## Proposed Solution
Standardize on one variable name (preferably `--color-background` as it is more descriptive) and update all usages of `--color-bg`.

title: Standardize component function declaration style
description:
The codebase mixes function declaration styles for components:

## Arrow functions used in:
- App.tsx: `const App = () => (...)`
- Footer.tsx: `const Footer = () => {...}`
- SeasonalLogo.tsx: `const SeasonalLogo = ({...}) => {...}`

## Function declarations used in:
- Header.tsx: `export default function Header() {...}`
- Home.tsx: `export default function Home() {...}`
- About.tsx, Crops.tsx, Contact.tsx

## Proposed Solution
Per project standards, standardize on function declarations for top-level components.

title: Lambda Node.js 24 Runtime Upgrade
description:
Upgrade the contact form Lambda function from Node.js 20 (EOL) to Node.js 24.

## Background
Node.js 20 has reached end-of-life and the Lambda runtime needs to be upgraded to Node.js 24. The console has already been updated; these issues track the code changes needed to align the infrastructure-as-code.

## Scope
- SAM template runtime specification
- TypeScript type definitions
- TypeScript compiler target
- Testing and validation

## Files Affected
- lambda/template.yaml
- lambda/package.json
- lambda/tsconfig.json

title: Update SAM template runtime to nodejs24.x
description:
Update the AWS SAM template to specify Node.js 24 runtime.

## File
`lambda/template.yaml` line 8

## Change
```yaml
# Before
Runtime: nodejs20.x

# After
Runtime: nodejs24.x
```

## Notes
- The console has already been updated to Node.js 24
- This change aligns the infrastructure-as-code with the deployed runtime

title: Update @types/node to v24
description:
Update the TypeScript type definitions for Node.js 24.

## File
`lambda/package.json` line 16

## Change
```json
// Before
"@types/node": "^20.0.0"

// After
"@types/node": "^24.0.0"
```

## Notes
- Ensures TypeScript type definitions match the runtime version
- Run `npm install` after updating to fetch new types

title: Update TypeScript target to ES2024
description:
Update the TypeScript compiler target to ES2024 to leverage newer JavaScript features available in Node.js 24.

## File
`lambda/tsconfig.json` line 3

## Change
```json
// Before
"target": "ES2020"

// After
"target": "ES2024"
```

## Optional Enhancement
Also update the `lib` option to match:
```json
"lib": ["ES2024"]
```

## Notes
- Node.js 24 supports ES2024 features natively
- This is optional but allows use of newer language features
- No breaking changes expected in existing code

title: Test Lambda after Node.js 24 upgrade
description:
Validate that the Lambda function works correctly after the Node.js 24 upgrade.

## Testing Checklist
- [ ] Build the Lambda with `npm run build`
- [ ] Deploy using `sam deploy` (or existing deployment method)
- [ ] Test contact form submission end-to-end
- [ ] Verify SES emails are sent correctly
- [ ] Check CloudWatch logs for any runtime errors or warnings
- [ ] Confirm reCAPTCHA verification still works

## Potential Issues to Watch
- Native `fetch` API (introduced in Node 18) should continue to work
- AWS SDK v3 compatibility with Node.js 24
- Any deprecated APIs that may have been removed

## Rollback
If issues are found, the console can be reverted to Node.js 20 while investigating
